commit.message:
Implement milestone encounters (Roadmap Step 13) (#34)

Add milestone encounter system — per-discipline tougher encounters that
reward 50%-improved CardEffects and offer escalating tier progression.

Key features:
- 5 discipline milestones: Combat, Mining, Herbalism, Woodcutting, Fishing
- Exponential insight cost: 100 * 2^(tier-1) MilestoneInsight
- Win → 50% better CardEffects + MilestoneScouting (pick 1 of 3 next-tier)
- Loss → reset encounter, return card to hand, back to NoEncounter
- Dedicated milestone hand separate from regular encounters (max 5)
- Abort treated as loss (reset + NoEncounter)

Types added: MilestoneDef, MilestoneEncounterState, EncounterPhase::MilestoneScouting,
  ActionPayload::MilestonePickScoutingChoice, TokenType::MilestoneMaxHand
New module: src/library/disciplines/milestone.rs (~900 lines)
Tests: 9 integration tests in tests/milestone_tests.rs

Docs updated: roadmap step 13 marked implemented, death-scaling references
removed from vision.md/roadmap.md, designer.rs and hints.rs updated.

BREAKING: EncounterKind, EncounterState, EncounterPhase, ActionPayload,
TokenType enums all have new variants.

Rename SoRaytrace* API to generic SoScene*/SoSc* names (#389)

* Initial plan

* Rename SoRaytrace*/SoRt* API to SoScene*/SoSc* for generic rendering

The old names implied CPU raytracing exclusively; these classes are also
used by rasterization backends such as Vulkan.  Rename to reflect the
more generic purpose:

  SoRaytraceRenderAction   -> SoSceneRenderAction
  SoRaytracingParams       -> SoSceneRendererParams
  SoRaytracerSceneCollector-> SoSceneCollector
  SoRtMaterial             -> SoScMaterial
  SoRtTriangle             -> SoScTriangle
  SoRtLightType            -> SoScLightType
  SoRtLightInfo            -> SoScLightInfo
  SoRtTextOverlay          -> SoScTextOverlay
  SO_RT_DIRECTIONAL        -> SO_SC_DIRECTIONAL
  SO_RT_POINT              -> SO_SC_POINT
  SO_RT_SPOT               -> SO_SC_SPOT

- Create new files (headers + sources) for all three classes with
  updated names, guards, doc comments, and description text.
- Update all call sites: SoActions.h, SoNodes.h, SoAction.cpp,
  SoNode.cpp, SoProceduralShape.cpp, all CMakeLists.txt files,
  all test utilities and rendering tests, and both doc pages.
- Delete the six superseded files.

commit.message:
Use attention instead of hard coding items/enemies/projectiles (#174)

* Unified entity attention: replace separate enemy/item/projectile arrays with transformer self-attention

Replace three separate fixed-size entity arrays (enemies 4×6, items 2×4, projectiles 2×5) with a unified 12-slot entity list processed by self-attention. Each entity gets a 9-dim feature vector (presence, position, direction, health, stun, hurts_on_touch, killable) plus a learned type embedding for ~74 entity types.

Architecture changes:
- EntityAttentionEncoder: type embedding (8-dim) + linear projection + 1-layer TransformerEncoder (4 heads, ff=128) + mean pooling
- CombinedExtractor: image CNN (256) + entity attention (64) + boolean info (15)
- Handles all-empty entity slots gracefully (unmask dummy token to prevent NaN softmax)

Observation changes:
- Unified entities (12×9) and entity_types (MultiDiscrete) replace enemy_features/item_features/projectile_features
- Clock item support: sets enemy hurts_on_touch=0, stun=1.0 when active
- Added has_clock boolean (index 14), kept full_health (index 13)

Also updates:
- Debugger observation panel: entity list widget replacing vector circle grid
- Rollout buffer: MultiDiscrete observation support
- All tests updated and passing (678 pass)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty action mask crash: safety fallback + rich diagnostics

When Link is stuck (can_link_move returns False for all directions) AND
no enemies/items are present (sword/beams disabled), the flat action mask
can end up all-False. In multihead mode this produces an all-False action
type mask, causing a crash in Categorical distribution.

Fix:
- action_space.py: if flat mask is entirely empty, force-enable all MOVE
  directions as a safety valve so the agent can always act
- models.py: replace bare assert with ValueError that includes full mask
  values, action type mask, direction mask, and action space config for
  easier debugging if this ever triggers again

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace safety fallback with diagnostic crash on empty action mask

Link should always have at least one valid move direction. An empty mask
indicates a bug in can_link_move or tile walkability, not a normal edge
case to paper over. The RuntimeError now dumps: location, position, tile,
link status, per-direction can_link_move results, actions_possible,
invalid_actions from training hints, enemy/item counts, and the full flat
mask. The models.py ValueError also includes the obs info vector.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add GAE checkbox to debugger toolbar (off by default)

The GAE advantage recomputation was running on every step, slowing down
the debugger. Add a 'GAE' checkbox to the toolbar that controls whether
advantages are calculated. When unchecked (default), the Advantage column
is empty and no computation happens. Toggling on recomputes immediately;
toggling off clears all advantage values. The γ/λ spinboxes still trigger
recomputation only when GAE is enabled.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

zero or small rolled_values (HerbalismMatch, WoodcuttingChop, etc.).

as cost amounts instead of being multiplied by the effect's rolled_value.

fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the

candidates fail.

chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow (#154)

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls
the pinned image on the VPS, retags it as :latest, and restarts via
docker compose — without touching .env or docker-compose.yml.

Shares the deploy-vps concurrency group to prevent concurrent
deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

commit.message:
FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts to fix scene_texture crash (#380)

* Initial plan

* FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts for proper GL isolation

Fixes _mesa_ReadPixels crash in SoSceneTexture2P::updatePBuffer when the
Rendering/scene_texture demo is shown in the obol_viewer System GL panel.

Root cause: SoOffscreenRenderer sets SORTED_OBJECT_BLEND transparency which
forces updatePBuffer (not updateFrameBuffer). updatePBuffer needs real GL
context isolation — it assumes makeContextCurrent switches to a fresh inner
context so that glReadPixels sees only tmpFbo, not the outer renderer's FBO.
The old FLTKContextManager shared one GL context for everything, so the outer
FBO binding leaked into the inner "context", causing the Mesa crash.

Fix (X11/GLX): ensureWindow() now records mainGLCtx_ and mainDrawable_ after
the first make_current(). createOffscreenContext() creates a real GLXContext
via glXCreateContext(dpy, vi, mainGLCtx_, True) — sharing textures/VBOs with
the FLTK context but having isolated GL state. makeContextCurrent() uses
glXMakeCurrent() to actually switch; restorePreviousContext() switches back.
Windows/macOS fall back to the original single-context approach (unchanged).

Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Slice shape inference crashes with SIGABRT on empty dimensions (#7738)

Fix #7735 

This pull request addresses issues with the ONNX Slice operator when
handling empty dimensions, ensuring correct and safe shape inference in
these edge cases. The main focus is to prevent undefined behavior and
ensure that slicing on empty dimensions consistently produces outputs
with zero length, regardless of the step direction. Additional
regression tests are included to verify these fixes, and several test
data files are updated to reflect the new behavior.

**Slice operator improvements for empty dimensions:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to
short-circuit and produce a zero-length output when the input dimension
size is zero, avoiding invalid clamp bounds and potential undefined
behavior.
* Modified shape inference logic in `old.cc` to detect empty dimensions
and set the output dimension value to zero, both for standard and
expanded shape inference code paths.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR2635-R2645)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR6329-R6335)

**Testing and regression coverage:**

* Added two new tests in `shape_inference_test.py` to verify that
slicing on empty dimensions with both positive and negative steps
produces the correct output shape, preventing regression of issue #7735.

**Test data updates:**

* Updated several `output_0.pb` binary test data files under
`onnx/backend/test/data/node/` (including attention, gelu,
hammingwindow, and lppool tests) to align with the corrected Slice
operator behavior on empty dimensions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-875055c843db41ea7d579c8bd29201e6f60bb4175ac96f840bdd27d488866198L1-R7)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d6fe5631baa8b6cb320c03143f797abe3dce4ddf5363fa9cfe71cc12bc31ac53L2-R3)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3275d5e3517a0e95967de3c46a75b02104980f2566f1a0cbefa386ea8e02f312L1-R3)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-537fe44987ef880d522c9c88283070745874abc990951f49eff8dccc35630af1L2-R2)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3d57e2f0cf63b32349f6619a2f61e90d4a4aa4fdac9a794af6bfb00c97fe9f8eL1-R2)

These changes collectively improve the robustness of ONNX shape
inference and operator correctness when handling tensors with empty
dimensions.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Add SystemVerilog testbenches for RTL primitives library

Create 16 standalone testbench files in testbenches/ directory covering:
- CDC: sync_2ff, async_fifo
- Arbiters: fixed_priority_arbiter, round_robin_arbiter
- Bitops: priority_encoder, popcount, barrel_shifter
- Counters: up_counter, gray_counter
- Memory: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo
- Handshake: skid_buffer
- Pipeline: pipeline_register, delay_line
- Encoding: gray_encoder
- Clock: clock_divider

Each testbench uses `timescale 1ns/1ps, waveform dumps, $error for
failures, PASS/FAIL summary, and tests basic functionality plus corner
cases such as reset, overflow, backpressure, and one-bit Gray transitions.
Testbenches assume DUT modules are compiled separately in the same
compilation unit.

commit.message:
Add memory RTL primitives: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo, register_file, cam, line_buffer, circular_buffer

- dual_port_ram: true dual-port RAM with independent clocks, registered-read for block RAM inference
- simple_fifo: synchronous FIFO with explicit count register for full/empty detection
- register_file: multi-read-port file with combinational reads and write-to-read forwarding
- content_addressable_memory: fully-associative CAM with priority-encoded hit address
- line_buffer: streaming image line buffer outputting a vertical column of NUM_LINES pixels
- circular_buffer: ring buffer with head/tail pointers, full/empty, and count output

All modules: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE parameters, always_ff/always_comb,
no initial blocks, no vendor primitives, fully synthesizable.

commit.message:
Debugger fixes (#173)

* Fix keyboard input not working until F5 in debugger

Intercept ShortcutOverride events for arrow/A keys in the application
event filter so focused widgets (QTreeView, QComboBox) cannot claim
them for their own navigation, preventing the subsequent KeyPress from
reaching our game controls.

Also give the game view StrongFocus and shift focus to it after bridge
creation so the model browser tree doesn't hold focus unnecessarily.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove 'Scenario:' label from debugger toolbar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add GAE advantage column to debugger step history

Add an Advantage column between Action and Reward in the step history
tree. On each step, extract the model's value estimate and recompute
GAE advantages for all buffered steps using the standard formula:
  δ_t = r_t + γ·V(s_{t+1})·mask - V(s_t)
  A_t = δ_t + γ·λ·mask·A_{t+1}

Toolbar spinboxes for γ and λ default to the training hyperparameters
from ml_ppo.py (0.99, 0.95). Changing them triggers recomputation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add multi-select and Ctrl+C copy to step history

Change selection mode from SingleSelection to ExtendedSelection so
multiple rows can be selected with Shift/Ctrl+click. Ctrl+C copies
all selected rows as tab-separated text (all columns) to the clipboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Renormalize probability column and add unmasked column

PROBABILITY column now excludes masked actions when computing
percentages, so visible values always sum to 100%. New UNMASKED
column shows raw softmax probabilities from the model.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Load State to debugger File menu

File > Load State (Ctrl+L) opens a .state file picker, resets the
environment, injects the loaded emulator state bytes, and steps a
noop to re-derive all wrapper state. The scenario remains active
for critics, end conditions, and objectives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix multihead direction mask ignoring action constraints

flat_mask_to_multihead() was hardcoding the direction mask to all True,
meaning multihead models could sample any direction regardless of which
directions were actually valid. This affected training, evaluation, and
the debugger equally.

The direction mask is now the union of valid directions across all valid
action types. A direction is only unmasked if at least one enabled action
type allows it. This prevents e.g. MOVE N when only MOVE S is walkable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Print exit criteria metric value after each training leg

After each scenario in a training circuit, print the last observed
value of the exit criteria metric alongside the target threshold and
whether it was reached. Helps diagnose training progress without
needing to check tensorboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add descriptive tooltips to γ and λ labels and spinboxes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Suppress too-many-return-statements on _can_move_from (R0911)
